pages.title: About - Jon Trickett
pages.meta_description: A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency
headings: h2: Jon Trickett A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency
pages.text_excerpt: About - Jon Trickett Join Get Involved Donate Jon Trickett A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency About Contact Jon in Parliament What Jon Thinks Search Search Twitter Facebook Youtube About Jon Trickett is the Labour MP for Normanton & Hemsworth Constituency, having been the Member of Parliament since 1996, representing former mining communities in the heart of Yorkshire. Prior to being elected to Parliament, Jon had been a councillor for Beeston ward in South Leeds, elected to the council in the middle of the miners’ strike in 1984, and became Leader of the Leeds Council in 1989. Jon was Leader of the Council during the years when Mrs Thatcher’s premiership was at its most powerful. The Council resisted the impact of many of Mrs Thatcher’s policies in an effort to protect communities. The City Council stood for economic growth and strong communities and in spite of the government’s efforts, created thousands of new jobs within Leeds whilst equally avoiding the necessity to introduce compulsory redundancies. Under Jon’s leadership the City of Leeds pioneered new forms of social partnership and innovative ways of providing public services. Entering Parliament in a by-election in 1996, Jon saw the final months of the decaying Tory Government. In 1997 Jon joined the Labour Government briefly as PPS to Peter Mandelson and was witness to many of the early actions of the Labour Government. Jon spent most of the rest of the years of Tony Blair’s premiership on the back benches where he spoke out on a range of progressive issues. A Labour loyalist but with a strong independent voice, Jon did not hesitate to criticise actions which he felt were damaging to the interests of his constituents and working people. Jon was particularly concerned about the marketisation of public services, the introduction of tuition fees, and the war in Iraq. After the 2005 election, Jon produced an analysis of the election results which indicated that unless the Labour Government reoriented its policies, then there was a real danger that the subsequent election would be a very difficult one for Labour. Jon chaired the Compass group of MPs and was the chair of the campaign to elect Jon Cruddas as Deputy Leader of the Labour Party. In 2008, Jon re-entered the Labour Government as PPS to the Prime Minister, Gordon Brown and served in No 10 Downing Street until the 2010 election. After the election of Ed Miliband as Leader of the Labour Party Jon became a full member of the Shadow Cabinet and is currently Deputy Chair of the Labour Party and Shadow Minister without Portfolio where his overriding preoccupation is the construction of a mass party of working people. Under the new leadership of Jeremy Corbyn, in 2015 Jon was appointed Shadow Secretary of State for Communities and Local Government and Shadow Minister for the Constitutional Convention. In the reshuffle in the summer of 2016 he was made Shadow Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy and Chair of Campaign and Election Strategy. pages.title: Contact - Jon Trickett
pages.meta_description: A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency
headings: h2: Jon Trickett A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency
pages.text_excerpt: Contact - Jon Trickett Join Get Involved Donate Jon Trickett A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency About Contact Jon in Parliament What Jon Thinks Search Search Twitter Facebook Youtube Contact My policy is to fight hard to get justice for local people. I will be fearless in this work and I am ready to do what it takes to get the best for our area. The Normanton & Hemsworth constituency office handles a large volume of casework and I employ a great team of staff to help constituents with any problems that they have. This office operates an open-door policy, where any constituent can come in and talk to a member of staff. For Covid safety and security reasons, please call ahead of your visit to book an appointment so that we can be ready to help. Our public office opening hours are from 9.30 am-4:00pm, Monday-Friday. If you have a case which you need advice on please contact us on: Telephone: 01977 655695 Email: jtrickett@jontrickett.org.uk Address: Room G12, Moorthorpe Railway Station, Barnsley Road, South Kirkby, WF9 3AT Social channels Twitter Facebook Youtube Further details Promoted by and on behalf of the Labour Party at Southside, 105 Victoria Street, London SW1E 6QT ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Pension age petition - Jon Trickett
pages.meta_description: A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency
headings: h2: Jon Trickett A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency
h3: Stop any attempts to increase state pension age to 75
pages.text_excerpt: Pension age petition - Jon Trickett Join Get Involved Donate Jon Trickett A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency About Contact Jon in Parliament What Jon Thinks Search Search Twitter Facebook Youtube Pension age petition Former Conservative Department of Work and Pensions Secretary, Iain Duncan Smith MP, recently argued that the state pension age should be increased to 75. This is a disgrace to all hardworking people who have deservedly earned their state pension. Labour is opposed to any increase in the state pension age, and Labour government will set up a commission to review the pension age and examine the possibility of a flexible retirement policy. A Labour government would also guarantee the triple lock pension, and protect your Winter Fuel Allowance and your free bus pass. Please sign this petition to say no to the government ever increasing the state pension age to 75. Stop any attempts to increase state pension age to 75 Name * First Last Email Address * Street Address Address Line 2 City Post Code * I support the Labour Party Yes Consent * I agree to the privacy policy. Your personal information will be kept private and held securely. It will not be shared with any third-party or used for any other purpose than this petition. Share — Social channels Twitter Facebook Youtube Further details Promoted by and on behalf of the Labour Party at Southside, 105 Victoria Street, London SW1E 6QT ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: The Big Issues survey – let us know what you think - Jon Trickett
pages.meta_description: A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency
headings: h2: Jon Trickett A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency
pages.text_excerpt: The Big Issues survey – let us know what you think - Jon Trickett Join Get Involved Donate Jon Trickett A strong Yorkshire voice for Hemsworth constituency About Contact Jon in Parliament What Jon Thinks Search Search Twitter Facebook Youtube The Big Issues survey – let us know what you think Jon Trickett MP From the many emails, letters, telephone calls as well as social media contacts I receive, it is clear people in our area are suffering as a result of 9 years of Tory austerity, yet the Government tell us things are getting better. People definitely don’t feel it around here. I have put together this survey for residents of Hemsworth Constituency and hope you will help by letting me know exactly what your big concerns are and what you think about Labour’s pledges. I hope you will take time to fill it in. With best wishes, Jon Trickett MP The Big Issues Survey Name * Street Address * Town * Postcode * Email Cost of living The price of many everyday services and purchases are rising faster than wages and income. What would you say has affected you the most? (Please tick all that apply) Food Gas/Electricity Housing Costs Petrol/Diesel Bus/Train Fares Childcare If you've been adversely affected by the rise in the State Pension Age tick this box Impacts of Austerity As we all know, Tory cuts have affected all of us in the North unfairly, which cuts have affected you? NHS Cuts Council Cuts Cuts to Welfare payments Education Cuts Cuts to Transport services Police Cuts Plans to cancel Pensioners Free TV licences Winter Fuel Allowance Cuts Pledge 1: Increased funding to the NHS to help employ more nurses and doctors The NHS is in Crisis. It is the fault of this Tory government and the failures of privatisation causing increasing A&E waiting times, fewer beds and not enough doctors and nurses. Labour will change that. We will give the NHS the funding it deserves to make sure you get the best treatment. I agree I disagree Not sure Pledge 2: A Real Living wage of £10 Labour will guarantee a real living wage of £10 as the minimum wage for everyone, including young people. This will provide the economic security people round here need. I agree I disagree I am not sure A Green New Deal that will create 400,000 jobs The climate is facing an emergency and Labour want to be part of the solution. In government we want to implement a Green New Deal that will boost our economy as well as help our environment with sustinable industries and promote renewable energy so that future generations can prosper. I agree I disagree I am not sure Pledge 4: Free School Meals Every child deserves a good meal whilst at school. That is vital to their education and development. I agree I disagree I am not sure Pledge 5: Protect the Winter fuel allowance, free TV licences and bus passes for pensioners The Tories keep toying with scrapping vital concessions that vulnerable elderly people rely on. Labour would never do that. We believe pensioners who have worked hard all their life deserve the dignity they have earnt. We will protect the Winter Fuel Allowance, free TV licences and buss passes I agree I disagree I am not sure A future General Election If there was a general election tomorrow, which political party would you vote for? If you agreed with the Labour Party's pledges then vote Labour to make them a reality Labour Conservative Lib Dem Green Ukip Other 2017 General Election Which political party did you vote for at the 2017 General Election? Labour Conservative Lib Dem Independent Other Didn't Vote Consent * Thank you for filling out my survey. Your personal information will be kept private and held securely. It will not be shared with any third-party or used for any other purpose than this petition. I agree to the privacy policy Share — Social channels Twitter Facebook Youtube Further details Promoted by and on behalf of the Labour Party at Southside, 105 Victoria Street, London SW1E 6QT ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
